ddls:
CREATE TABLE "web_accounts" (
  "account_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each web account */,
  "label" TEXT /* Label or name of the web service */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the web service */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the web account */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the web account (encrypted) */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the web page (e.g., login page title) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the account was last updated */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the web account */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the web account (e.g., social media, e-commerce) */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the account is active */,
  "last_used" TEXT /* Timestamp when the account was last used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("account_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, suspended, deleted) */,
  "last_password_change" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last password change */,
  "failed_login_attempts" INTEGER /* Number of failed login attempts */,
  "lockout_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account is locked out */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Detailed permissions for the role in JSON format */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_web_accounts" (
  "user_account_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-web account mapping */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "account_id" INTEGER /* ID of the web account */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the user's access to the web account */,
  "access_start_date" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user's access to the web account started */,
  "access_end_date" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user's access to the web account ends */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_account_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "target_table" TEXT /* Name of the table affected */,
  "target_id" INTEGER /* ID of the row affected */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Details of the action */,
  "action_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action was performed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client used to perform the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "backup_records" (
  "backup_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each backup record */,
  "backup_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the backup was taken */,
  "backup_file_path" TEXT /* File path to the backup file */,
  "backup_size" REAL /* Size of the backup file in MB */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the backup (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "backup_type" TEXT /* Type of the backup (e.g., full, incremental) */,
  "backup_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the backup process in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("backup_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "recovery_records" (
  "recovery_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each recovery record */,
  "recovery_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the recovery was performed */,
  "backup_id" INTEGER /* ID of the backup used for recovery */,
  "recovery_file_path" TEXT /* File path to the recovery file */,
  "recovery_size" REAL /* Size of the recovery file in MB */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the recovery (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "recovery_type" TEXT /* Type of the recovery (e.g., full, partial) */,
  "recovery_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the recovery process in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("recovery_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mfa_tokens" (
  "token_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each MFA token */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the token */,
  "token_value" TEXT /* Value of the MFA token */,
  "token_type" TEXT /* Type of the MFA token (e.g., SMS, email, app) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the token was created */,
  "expires_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the token expires */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the MFA token has been verified */,
  "verification_code" TEXT /* Verification code sent to the user for MFA */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("token_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "password_strength" (
  "strength_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each password strength rating */,
  "account_id" INTEGER /* ID of the web account */,
  "password_strength" TEXT /* Strength rating of the password (e.g., weak, medium, strong) */,
  "last_checked" TEXT /* Timestamp when the password strength was last checked */,
  "score" INTEGER /* Numerical score representing the strength of the password */,
  "next_check" TEXT /* Timestamp of the next scheduled check for password strength */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("strength_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "integration_services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each integrated service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the integrated service */,
  "api_key" TEXT /* API key for the service */,
  "api_secret" TEXT /* API secret for the service */,
  "enabled" INTEGER /* Status of the integration (e.g., enabled, disabled) */,
  "last_synced" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last synchronization */,
  "service_url" TEXT /* URL of the integrated service */,
  "last_sync_status" TEXT /* Status of the last synchronization (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "sync_frequency" INTEGER /* Synchronization frequency in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "account_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history entry */,
  "account_id" INTEGER /* ID of the web account */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "change_details" TEXT /* Details of the change */,
  "change_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the change was made */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the change was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client used to make the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id")
)
